title: boesenbergia flavorubra
intro: Boesenbergia flavorubra, also known as Krachai Dam or Thai Ginger, is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and lowering blood pressure, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Boesenbergia flavorubra is often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a fresh ingredient in soups and stir-fries.
